question: How many at bats did the Yankee with the most walks in the 1977 regular season have that same season?

research_queries: New York Yankees 1977 season statistics walks leaders
Yankees 1977 regular season most walks player at bats
MLB 1977 Yankees team statistics walks at bats
1977 New York Yankees roster batting stats
working_plan: Search for 1977 Yankees season batting statistics
Identify the Yankees player with the most walks in 1977 regular season
Find that specific player's at-bat total for the 1977 season
Use code to verify/sort data if needed
Cross-reference multiple sources to confirm accuracy

solver.reasoning_summary: Found 1977 Yankees hitting statistics showing Roy White led the team with 75 walks in the regular season. From the same data source, Roy White had 519 at-bats in 1977. Verified through code analysis of the complete team statistics and confirmed with StatMuse result specifically stating Roy White had the most walks for Yankees in 1977.

arguments.code:
# Let me parse the 1977 Yankees hitting data to find the player with most walks
# and then get their at-bats

hitting_data = [
    ("Alston, Dell", 22, 40, 10, 13, 4, 0, 1, 4, 3),
    ("Bergman, Dave", 5, 4, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0, 1, 0),
    ("Blair, Paul", 83, 164, 20, 43, 4, 3, 4, 25, 9),
    ("Chambliss, Chris", 157, 600, 90, 172, 32, 6, 17, 90, 45),
    ("Dent, Bucky", 158, 477, 54, 118, 18, 4, 8, 49, 39),
    ("Healy, Fran", 27, 67, 10, 15, 5, 0, 0, 7, 6),
    ("Hendricks, Elrod", 10, 11, 1, 3, 1, 0, 1, 5, 0),
    ("Jackson, Reggie", 146, 525, 93, 150, 39, 2, 32, 110, 74),
    ("Johnson, Cliff", 56, 142, 24, 42, 8, 0, 12, 31, 20),
    ("Kingman, Dave", 8, 24, 5, 6, 2, 0, 4, 7, 2),
    ("Klutts, Mickey", 5, 15, 3, 4, 1, 0, 1, 4, 2),
    ("Locklear, Gene", 1, 5, 1, 3, 0, 0, 0, 2, 0),
    ("May, Carlos", 65, 181, 21, 41, 7, 1, 2, 16, 17),
    ("Munson, Thurman", 149, 595, 85, 183, 28, 5, 18, 100, 39),
    ("Nettles, Graig", 158, 589, 99, 150, 23, 4, 37, 107, 68),
    ("Perez, Marty", 1, 4, 0, 2, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0),
    ("Piniella, Lou", 103, 339, 47, 112, 19, 3, 12, 45, 20),
    ("Randolph, Willie", 147, 551, 91, 151, 28, 11, 4, 40, 64),
    ("Rivers, Mickey", 138, 565, 79, 184, 18, 5, 12, 69, 18),
    ("Stanley, Fred", 48, 46, 6, 12, 0, 0, 1, 7, 8),
    ("White, Roy", 143, 519, 72, 139, 25, 2, 14, 52, 75),
    ("Wynn, Jimmy", 30, 77, 7, 11, 2, 1, 1, 3, 15),
    ("Zeber, George", 25, 65, 8, 21, 3, 0, 3, 10, 9),
]

# Format: (Name, G, AB, R, H, 2B, 3B, HR, RBI, BB)
# BB is walks (position 8), AB is at-bats (position 1)

print("Player walks analysis:")
walks_data = [(name, ab, walks) for name, g, ab, r, h, db, tp, hr, rbi, walks in hitting_data if walks > 0]
walks_data_sorted = sorted(walks_data, key=lambda x: x[2], reverse=True)

for name, ab, walks in walks_data_sorted:
    print(f"{name}: {walks} walks, {ab} at-bats")

print(f"\nPlayer with most walks: {walks_data_sorted[0][0]}")
print(f"Walks: {walks_data_sorted[0][2]}")  
print(f"At-bats: {walks_data_sorted[0][1]}")
